reasoning: The question asks if T's market close price will be higher than its 2025-10-26 close on future resolution dates. Generally, individual low-beta, high-dividend stocks like AT&T have a roughly 51-54% probability of being higher over short-to-medium term horizons (such as 1 to 6 months). Because T pays a substantial dividend, its price return drift is slightly lower than non-dividend-paying stocks of similar total return characteristics. In the absence of specific known negative catalysts or resolution date timelines, I anchor to a 52% base rate for a higher price.
